subject: Mathematics
title: Emmy Noether: Mother of Modern Algebra
description: A 60-second lesson on Emmy Noether: Mother of Modern Algebra.

hook.text: Einstein called her the most important woman in the history of mathematics.

buildup.text: Emmy Noether transformed abstract algebra by studying the deep structure of mathematical systems.

discovery.text: Her theorem linking symmetry to conservation laws became a cornerstone of modern physics.

twist.text: She taught at a German university for years without pay because women weren't allowed to hold chairs.

climax.text: Fleeing the Nazis, she moved to the US and died at 53 from surgery complications.

punchline.text: She worked without pay and reshaped two entire fields.
